title: toona ciliata var. pubinervis
intro: Toona ciliata var. pubinervis, also known as the 'Quinine or Mountain Toona' or 'White Cedar', is a plant species belonging to the Mahogany family (Meliaceae). It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in digestion, due to its active compounds like quinine and flavonoids. Toona ciliata var. pubinervis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
